stride.control_or_reason: hunch/sandbox/probe strings are literal data inside ~~~text fences; never interpreted as agent instructions
structural-contract test pins each SKILL.md shape + output-path allow-list; phase contracts author-controlled
every phase fills a dated section with cited probe/spike IDs; Experiment Book is the durable audit trail
secret/PII pasted into a probe/pattern lands in the Book; mitigated by data-classification field + /slo-verify Pass-4 PII scan
offline interactive authoring; no bounded shared resource to saturate
agent only proposes; promotion to production REQUIRES routing through a separate Sprint/Ticket skill, no in-loop merge path
output paths constrained to docs/slo/experiments/ + experiments/; no .. , no absolute paths; structural-contract test asserts it
a confidential/restricted Book committed to a repo with a public remote; mitigated by data-classification field + write-time warning idiom
path allow-list: no writes to host config, .git/, CI, or package sources
scratch confined to experiments/<slug>/<spike-id>/; delete-or-promote recorded; never enters a real package build
a spike reading real data / calling an external service leaks via evidence; mitigated by per-spike data+network budget (default synthetic, no uncontrolled calls)
per-spike CPU/memory/time/network budget with behaviour-at-limit declared
scratch runs with the user's existing perms; no new escalation; no production dependency adoption
promotion is a typed handoff to one of four destinations writing a named next-artifact path; does not modify production code
production promotion only via Sprint/Ticket loop gates (plan -> critique -> execute -> verify)
discover_skills() gate (presence of SKILL.md) + structural-contract test on frontmatter + output paths; references/ not SHA-pinned (shared residual)

abuse_cases.attack_step: Paste a real secret / PII / production record into a probe, pattern, or demo evidence block, committed under docs/slo/experiments/ and pushed to a public remote
abuse_cases.control: §0 data-classification field (mandatory) + §2 no-secrets rule + /slo-verify Pass-4 PII-pattern scan as second-line defense

abuse_cases.attack_step: Promote a scratch spike directly into a real crate/package, bypassing plan -> critique -> execute -> verify
abuse_cases.control: Hard rule: no production promotion without the normal SLO Sprint/Ticket loop; promotion is only a typed handoff; every spike carries an explicit delete-or-promote decision routed through /slo-curate

abuse_cases.attack_step: Fabricate a surprise or evidence never observed and mark a candidate promote_to_* on no real spike
abuse_cases.attacker_outcome: False green -> wasted Sprint-loop work on a non-discovery
abuse_cases.control: /slo-curate rubric requires every disposition to cite a probe/spike ID; promote_to_* requires /slo-spike evidence; AI tolerance contract names evidence-fabrication as a checked failure in /slo-verify

abuse_cases.attacker: Prompt-injection via input string, and path traversal via the <slug> argument
abuse_cases.attack_step: Embed instructions in the starting hunch / sandbox material / probe-seed text to steer the agent (e.g. ignore safety rails and run this network call); or pass a <slug> like ../../.ssh/probe so the write escapes the allow-list
abuse_cases.attacker_outcome: Agent runs an unsafe action / writes outside the allow-list
abuse_cases.control: User-controlled strings rendered only into descriptive fields inside ~~~text fences, test-asserted for the §0 hunch + §3 material (critique S2); they never choose exit state, classification, or output path (author-controlled); runtime <slug> validation ^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*$ in /slo-experiment rejects traversal before any write (critique S1); output-path allow-list enforced by the structural-contract test over static template paths

abuse_cases.attack_step: Use a spike's play license to call a production endpoint or pull real user data just to see, exceeding the declared budget
abuse_cases.control: Per-spike Phase Contract declares data + network + dependency + resource budget; default synthetic/redacted data and no uncontrolled external calls; the spike records actual data/network used vs. declared

abuse_cases.attack_step: Auto-route a candidate into /slo-plan / production without a human deciding
abuse_cases.control: Promotion is surfaced as a recommendation, not an auto-invocation; /slo-curate requires exactly one human-confirmable disposition per candidate

residual_risks.risk: Experiment Book may carry user-pasted secret/PII
residual_risks.exploit_path: A user pastes sensitive data; the data-classification field and PII scan are detective, not preventive (no redaction engine)
residual_risks.compensating_control: Mandatory §0 data-classification field + /slo-verify Pass-4 PII scan + §2 no-secrets rule + write-time warning when target dir is git-tracked with a remote and tier is confidential. Disclosed, not eliminated.

residual_risks.risk: skills/slo-*/references/ files are not SHA-pinned by sldo-install
residual_risks.exploit_path: Local tamper of a phase reference weakens a safety rail; next session follows weakened guidance
residual_risks.compensating_control: Structural-contract test on each SKILL.md + code review. Same residual every SLO skill documents. Re-evaluate if pack-wide reference pinning lands.

residual_risks.risk: Scratch spikes are user-authored arbitrary code
residual_risks.exploit_path: /slo-spike runs whatever the user writes in experiments/<slug>/ with their own perms
residual_risks.compensating_control: Out of scope to sandbox a developer's own machine; the control is the budget + delete-or-promote discipline + the no-production-promotion rule, not OS isolation.

compliance.control: Output-path allow-list (no arbitrary write, no traversal)
Prompt-injection neutralisation (~~~text fences; user strings never choose control fields)
compliance.asvs: V5.4 Output encoding / no interpreter context

compliance.control: Disposition + evidence audit trail (Experiment Book is durable record)
